diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support. How can I help you today?
[Caller] Hey, can you explain soft pity to me? My friend keeps talking about it and I'm lost.
[Agent] Absolutely. Soft pity is the probability boost that begins around pull 75 on the 5-star rate of a character or weapon banner. From pull 75 onwards, the chance of hitting a 5-star on each subsequent wish increases sharply, climbing until you reach hard pity at pull 90, which guarantees a 5-star.
[Caller] So it's not a flat chance the whole time?
[Agent] Correct. For the first 74 pulls on a 5-star banner, the base rate is low — under one percent per wish. Starting at pull 75 the rate ramps up aggressively, so most 5-stars statistically drop between pulls 75 and 89, not at 90.
[Caller] Oh interesting. And what about the 50/50 thing?
[Agent] On character event banners, when you do hit the 5-star, there's a 50/50 chance it's the featured character versus a standard-pool 5-star. If you lose the 50/50, the next 5-star you pull on that banner is guaranteed to be the featured character.
[Caller] Okay, that's really helpful. Thank you.
[Agent] You're welcome! Happy wishing. Anything else?
[Caller] No, that's it.
[Agent] Have a great day!
